mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through improved profit expectations and reduced recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 473808.0M as of May 2017 reflects sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS momentum fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.5% signals tight labor markets and wage pressure, which equity markets interpret as inflationary risk and reduced real returns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.3% as of May 2017 is near historical lows
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when UNRATE < 4.5%, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 1.5% signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice forward earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.91% as of May 2017 is well below trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in < 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with stable or declining inflation (CPIAUCSL growth < 2.5% annualized) signals healthy supply-side expansion without overheating, supporting corporate profit margins and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.03 (2017=100) and CPIAUCSL at 243.85 show stable supply conditions
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth with low inflation fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10% month-over-month, signaling weakening construction demand and forward-looking economic pessimism, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1172.0K units as of April 2017
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in < 50% of instances following > 10% HOUST declines, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 200K jobs per month while UNRATE remains stable, signaling robust labor demand without wage inflation, supporting consumer spending and equity valuations with positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 146135.0K and UNRATE at 4.3% as of May 2017
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 57% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle slowdown and reduced corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices terminal growth rates.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16861.6B (2017 dollars) as of Q1 2017
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in < 50% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 2%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.5% annualized while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 1.5%, signaling negative real rates and central bank policy lag. This regime produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity investors seek inflation hedges.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 243.85 and FEDFUNDS at 0.91% as of May 2017
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal fails to produce positive 5-day returns in > 56% of triggered instances.
